verdict.note: Visual refinement re-measure (CSS-only: triangle 9->13px, slot 12->17px, +6px lead gutter). Injected-board Playwright render of a parent (Supervisor fleet) + 2 children on BOTH surfaces (console SessionInterface + map SessionWindow): collapsed hides children behind a now-noticeably-bigger disclosure triangle whose text sits clearly right of it (parent rows visibly distinct from the flush-left plain row); expanding flips triangle and reveals children indented+aligned beneath. Fold/rollup LOGIC (incl. auto-promote-on-close) unchanged by this edit and not re-exercised.

Injected-board render (CSS-only visual refinement; colours/logic untouched): Supervisor-fleet parent with a working child + an asking child paints a DARK-YELLOW disclosure triangle (any-descendant-needs-attention), yet the parent stays in the RUNNING/self-running zone with its own parked (||) glyph — the downward rollup did NOT move its zone or sort. Bigger triangle just makes the hue easier to read; STATUS_COLOR rollup unchanged.

Re-tune re-measure (CSS-only, correcting the prior OVERSHOOT): .sess-fold slot 17->13px + font 13->12px, .sess-lead margin-right 6px->0 (killed the 6+7=13px double-gap). Measured on the REAL board (genuine spex-new children of 51c1520a) in a headless browser, BOTH surfaces sharing .sess-fold/.sess-lead. Console SessionInterface (.si-item): parent row carries a compact triangle; collapsed HIDES its two real children; clicking flips it and both children (Demo session for UI, Tune session-nesting) appear indented one depth level beneath, placeholder-aligned. Computed: foldWidth 13px, fontSize 12px, lead margin-right 0px, single 7px gap triangle->headline (was 13px), children headLeft = parent+14px so alignment holds. Fold/rollup/auto-promote LOGIC untouched by this pure size/spacing edit.

Re-tune re-measure (CSS-only; colours/logic untouched, only slot size shrank 17->13px & the 6px lead double-gap removed). REAL board: parent 51c1520a has a child that is 'asking' (needs-you) -> its disclosure triangle computes rgb(181,137,0) = DARK-YELLOW (any-descendant-needs-attention). Crucially the parent STILL sits in the RUNNING/self-running zone carrying its OWN parked glyph (rgb(133,153,0), parked bar), so the downward rollup did NOT move its zone or sort. The compact triangle reads the hue just as clearly at 13px/12px. STATUS_COLOR rollup + zone/sort logic unchanged by this pure size/spacing edit.

Real child spawned via spex new FROM THIS SESSION'S own process (74797bd8, parent auto-recorded). Collapsed: parent row leads with the FILLED fold pod counting 1, child hidden, parent glyph/zone its own. Click: pod turns OUTLINE, child indented beneath. Sibling proof in-frame: the proposal session shows a filled ③ (3 descendants). Auto-promote leg not re-run (closing PARENT = closing myself); prior reading covers it.

Pod colour = the rollup, verified live: with the child in needs-attention the collapsed pod filled DARK-YELLOW rgb(181,137,0) (STATUS_COLOR.asking) while the parent kept its own green working glyph and self-running zone — passive hint, no escalation. Single-child run (spec asks two mixed states — the yellow-wins branch is the one exercised); expanded pod keeps the colour as outline+number.
Real browser: expanding real parent showed children indented under it; closing a throwaway parent left its child visible top-level with depth 0 on reload.
Real browser collapsed parent fold pod had rgb(181,137,0) yellow rollup from descendant needs-attention rows while parent retained its own row/glyph/zone.

Real browser on worktree dashboard (:5198) against live backend :8787. Collapsed/expanded measured on the real 51983f -> be4f pair after opening ancestor 3ec0: collapsed parent row showed filled fold pod '1' and be4f was absent from visible rows; click changed pod to outline/open and be4f appeared directly beneath with deeper connector rails (2 rails vs parent 1), while parent kept its own glyph/zone. Auto-promote measured in the same browser run on disposable e876 -> 8dd0 pair after dashboard close: e876 absent, 8dd0 visible top-level despite dangling parent id, with no rail/fold; zero page errors.
